entities.name: BAYESIAN MULTITRAIT KERNEL METHODS
entities.description: Models for genomic prediction that account for correlations between phenotypic traits and capture nonlinear patterns.

entities.name: GENOMIC PREDICTION
entities.description: The process of predicting phenotypic values based on genomic data.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC TRAITS
entities.description: Observable characteristics used as data for genomic prediction.

entities.name: LINEAR KERNEL
entities.description: A type of kernel used within Bayesian multitrait kernel methods.

entities.name: GAUSSIAN KERNEL
entities.description: A type of kernel used within Bayesian multitrait kernel methods that outperformed linear models.

entities.name: POLYNOMIAL KERNEL
entities.description: A type of kernel used within Bayesian multitrait kernel methods.

entities.name: SIGMOID KERNEL
entities.description: A type of kernel used within Bayesian multitrait kernel methods.

entities.name: RIDGE REGRESSION
entities.description: A conventional multitrait model used for genomic prediction comparison.

entities.name: GBLUP MULTITRAIT MODELS
entities.description: Conventional linear models used for genomic prediction.

entities.name: MEAN SQUARE ERROR OF PREDICTION
entities.description: A metric used to evaluate the prediction performance of genomic models.

entities.name: NONLINEAR PATTERNS
entities.description: Complex data relationships captured more efficiently by kernel methods than linear models.

entities.name: GENOMIC PREDICTION ACCURACY
entities.description: The measure of how well genomic prediction models perform.
